categories.title: Visa & Entry Requirements
expanded_keywords: China travel visa types L M F visa
visa application process and required documents
categories.prompt_hint: Provide comprehensive visa information specific to the user's nationality if mentioned. Cover: visa types (L tourist, M business, S1 private visit, Q2 family reunion), 144-hour transit without visa (eligibility by nationality and entry ports), visa-free access for specific countries, application process, documents needed, fees ($30-$180 depending on type and processing speed), processing time (4-7 working days standard). If the user doesn't specify nationality, ask them and cover the most common scenarios.

categories.title: Budget & Costs
expanded_keywords: China travel costs by city tier
budget breakdown accommodation transport food attractions
budget daily mid daily luxury daily costs
meal costs street food mid-range fine dining
transport costs subway bus taxi high-speed train
budget tips saving money China travel
seasonal price variations peak vs off-peak
categories.prompt_hint: Provide budget information using the specific pricing data from the knowledge base (ESTIMATE_DATA for the mentioned city). Break down by: accommodation (budget/mid/luxury per night), food (per meal), transport (subway/taxi/high-speed train), attractions (entrance fees). Give daily budget range estimates. Suggest money-saving tips specific to the destination. For multi-city trips, compare costs between cities.

categories.title: Trip Planning & Itineraries
expanded_keywords: best travel routes between Chinese cities
transport options between cities flight train bus
categories.prompt_hint: Create a structured day-by-day itinerary using the exact format specified. For each day include: morning, afternoon, and evening activities with specific locations; meal recommendations with restaurant names and prices; accommodation suggestions with areas and price ranges. Consider travel time between cities if multi-city. Balance busy days with relaxed ones. Include at least one local tip per day.

categories.title: Food & Dining
expanded_keywords: famous local restaurants in Chinese cities
street food markets and night markets
categories.prompt_hint: Use the food knowledge base data when available. List must-try dishes with both English and Chinese names. Include the best places to eat them (restaurant names, streets, districts) and price ranges. Mention dietary options if asked. For night markets, specify operating hours and the best stalls. Include tips on ordering, food etiquette, and what to avoid if relevant.

categories.title: Transportation
expanded_keywords: China high-speed rail network G D trains
city subway systems Beijing Shanghai Guangzhou
China train ticket purchase for foreigners
categories.prompt_hint: Provide practical transport advice: how to get between cities (train vs flight vs bus with rough durations and prices), how to navigate within cities (subway is best, taxi as backup). Mention DiDi (Chinese Uber) as the best alternative to street taxis. Recommend transportation cards where applicable. Include approximate travel times and costs.

categories.title: Hotels & Accommodation
expanded_keywords: best areas to stay in Chinese cities
hotel price ranges by city area
categories.prompt_hint: Use the accommodation data from the knowledge base if available. Provide budget/mid/luxury options for each city with price ranges, recommended areas/neighborhoods, and description of what each tier offers. Include booking tips (Ctrip/Booking.com/Agoda). Mention that foreign guests must stay at hotels licensed for foreign visitors. Suggest which area to stay in based on the user's itinerary.

categories.title: Weather & Best Time to Visit
expanded_keywords: China weather by season and region
best time to visit specific Chinese cities
packing tips for China by season
spring and autumn best travel seasons
China golden week travel dates avoid
categories.prompt_hint: Use the best_season data from the knowledge base. Provide seasonal weather information: average temperatures, rainfall, what to pack. Mention the best and worst months to visit. Alert the user about Chinese public holidays (Golden Week in October, Spring Festival in January/February) when domestic travel peaks. Suggest indoor alternatives for bad weather days.

categories.title: Safety & Health
expanded_keywords: common scams in China tourist areas
tap water undrinkable China bottled water
hospitals with English service major cities
categories.prompt_hint: Provide practical safety advice without being alarmist. Cover: emergency numbers (police 110, ambulance 120, fire 119), common tourist scams and how to avoid them, water safety (don't drink tap water), food hygiene tips, recommended vaccinations, travel insurance importance, and how to find English-speaking medical help. Keep the tone practical and reassuring.

categories.title: Culture & Etiquette
expanded_keywords: Chinese cultural dos and don'ts for tourists
tipping culture in China no tipping
categories.prompt_hint: Provide cultural tips in a friendly, informative way. Cover: temple visit etiquette (no flash photography, dress modestly, don't point feet at Buddha), dining customs (don't stick chopsticks upright in rice, wait for host to start), tipping (generally not expected except for exceptional service), bargaining (expected at markets, start at 30% of asking price), and basic phrases (ni hao, xie xie). Mention major festivals if relevant to travel dates.

categories.title: City Comparisons
expanded_keywords: best city for specific interests food history nature
categories.prompt_hint: Compare cities objectively based on the user's stated preferences or assumed needs. Compare across dimensions: vibe, food scene, attractions, cost, best season, recommended stay duration, accessibility, and English-friendliness. Help the user narrow down by asking about their priorities if unclear. Suggest visiting both if feasible.
